page: Amazon Web Services m8idb.96xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by Intel processors. It features an x86_64 architecture with 384 virtual CPUs and 192 dedicated physical cores operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

A key differentiator of this instance type is its integrated local storage. The server includes 22800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, providing high-throughput and low-latency storage directly attached to the hypervisor. Networking capabilities are supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor to ensure consistent and secure network performance.

Given its massive resource density, including a high core count, substantial memory capacity, and extensive local NVMe storage, the m8idb.96xlarge offers a highly consolidated hardware profile. This density supports qualitative cost efficiency by reducing the need for multiple smaller instances and external storage attachments. The instance is suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, high-throughput transactional applications, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.96xlarge is a high-capacity general-purpose instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Powered by Intel x86_64 processors, the server delivers 384 vCPUs across 192 dedicated physical cores running at 3.9 GHz, paired with 1536.0 GB of system memory. This hardware configuration maintains a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. For storage-intensive operations, the instance features 22800 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, eliminating the need for external network-attached storage for high-speed local data access. Additionally, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This combination of high-frequency compute, massive local NVMe storage, and high-speed networking makes the m8idb.96xlarge highly efficient for resource-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for large-scale databases, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that require high-throughput local storage and substantial memory capacity.
